title: Train An Ordered Hierarchical Target-Encoding Specialist For High-Cardinality Cats
summary: `Driver`, `Race`, and their interactions are high-cardinality, and regularized target encoding often beats crude frequency encoding when done out-of-fold.
rationale: `Driver`, `Race`, and their interactions are high-cardinality, and regularized target encoding often beats crude frequency encoding when done out-of-fold. Prior research already pointed toward leak-safe categorical priors; the concrete missing step is a strictly ordered, hierarchical backoff encoder rather than only counts/frequencies.
implementation_hint: Build cross-fitted or ordered encodings with shrinkage for `Driver`, `Race`, `Compound`, `Driver_Compound`, `Race_Compound`, `Driver_Stint`, and `Driver_Race` using chronology-aware folds. Back off hierarchically, e.g. `Driver_Race_Compound -> Driver_Race -> Driver -> global`, then train a simple LightGBM/XGBoost specialist or CatBoost model on raw cats plus these encodings.
expected_effect: Could unlock signal from repeat strategic tendencies and circuit-compound interactions that frequency features leave unused.
risk: Easy to leak if folds are wrong; over-shrinking may erase the benefit because many drivers/races are sparse.
